# 5.1.6 Recovering Mental Power

## **When a Monster Casts Magic on Adventurers** 

Each player must make a resistance roll using _monster's magic intensity_ as the target score. 

## **Example:** 

A centaur and three adventurers get into a fight. This centaur has the ability to use ancient magic and unleashes _Lightning_ . The game master declares that its magic intensity is _13_ . 

Each of the three adventurers (the players in control) roll 2D to check if they resist, using _their mental power resistance_ as the baseline score, and _13 (magic intensity)_ as the target score. 

## **When an Adventurer Casts Magic on Adventurers** 

The following procedure is used to make a check when data created for a character casts magic on adventurers, and vice versa. 

First, the side casting the magic rolls 2D. The final score (magic power + 2D) must then be determined this one time only. Each resisting side must then make a mental power resistance roll, using that determined final score as the target score. 

## **Example:** 

The evil sorcerer Gorman (magic power 5) unleashes _Lightning_ on the three adventurers. First, Gorman rolls 2D and adds his own magic power. If we assume his 2D roll is _8_ , each of the three adventurers must make a mental power resistance roll, with a target score of 5 (magic power) + 8 (roll) = _13_ . Suppose that the baseline score of all three of their mental power resistance rolls is _4_ . If their rolls are each _2_ (double ones!), _6_ , and _9_ , then only the _last_ one is able to resist the _Lightning_ . 

Handling double ones and double sixes is no different from when there’s only one target. If the side casting the magic rolls double ones, the magic has _no_ effect on _any_ targets. If the side casting the magic rolls double sixes, _no_ 

_one_ can resist, except for those who _also_ roll double sixes. 

## **When an Adventurer Casts Magic on a Mix of** 

## **Adventurers and Monsters** 

Sometimes enemies written in the same data format as a character may also be accompanied by monsters. For example, a sorcerer who leads undead monsters to attack villages. Casting magic on such a group is handled the same way as when an adventurer casts magic on (a group of) adventurers, as described above. At this time, the monsters must _also_ roll the dice to make resist checks. Please use the optional rules under _17.1: Changes to Magic Procedure_ (see p. 273). 

## **If the Target Does 5.1.4.8 Not Resist** 

Some magic can also have secondary effects. For example, the spirit magic _Fanaticism_ increases attack power, but conversely _decreases_ evasion speed. With such magic, the one on whom it is cast can _choose_ whether or not to resist. 

If the one on whom the magic is being cast chooses _not_ to resist the magic, it will _definitely_ take effect so long as the one casting the magic does _not_ roll double ones. You’re also allowed to choose to _not resist_ magic like _Fire Bolt_ , which will definitely result in a loss if it's cast on you....... but that probably won't benefit you in any way. 

## **5.1.5** 

## **Consuming Mental Power** 

Adventurers cannot use magic endlessly. When using magic, an adventurer must temporarily _reduce_ their mental power. 

Each magic has a score called **base mental power cost** . This base mental power cost, divided by the corresponding rune master skill level 

(rounded up), equals the actual mental power consumed by the character. 

## **Example:** 

A character with shaman skill level 2 uses the _Fire Bolt_ spell. The base mental power cost for _Fire Bolt_ is _5_ . The character must consume 5 ÷ 2 = 2.5, rounding up to _3_ points of mental power. 

A character can reduce their mental power to 0, but they _cannot_ make it negative. For example, a character with 5 points of mental power remaining _can_ use magic that costs 5 points of mental power, but _cannot_ use magic that costs _6 or more_ points of mental power. 

If a character’s mental power reaches _0_ , they will _lose consciousness_ . The character cannot regain consciousness unless they recover their mental power to _1 point or more_ in some way. 

Even if magic _fails_ , such as by rolling double ones or being resisted by the opponent, a character must _still_ consume their mental power, same as if they had actually used the magic. 

## **5.1.6** 

## **Recovering Mental Power** 

Mental power reduced due to using magic, etc. can be recovered via the following methods. 

## 《 **Magical Recovery** 》 

There's magic and magical items that can recover a character's mental power, such as the holy magic _Transfer Mental Power_ . The amount recovered is explained under each magic and item. 

## 《 **Recovery via Sleep** 》 

If a character sleeps for at least 6 hours in a row, they will fully recover their mental power upon awakening. However, this method of recovery can only be done _once_ a day. If you awaken 

60

# 5.1.7 Magic Expansion within 24 hours of your previous awakening (when your mental power was last recovered), your mental power will _not_ be recovered. ## **5.1.7** ## **Magic Expansion** You can increase the effectiveness and certainty of magic by consuming extra mental power. This is called **magic expansion** . Magic expansion is done by doubling or tripling...... the _amount_ of mental power consumed. At this time, you do _not_ double or triple the base mental power cost....... Instead, you divide the mental power by your _skill level, rounded up_ , or in other words, the mental power you would consume when _not_ expanding is doubled, tripled...... etc. ## **Example:** Suppose a sorcerer with sorcerer skill level 2 doubles the range of his _Energy Bolt_ spell and casts it on two enemies. The base mental power cost of _Energy Bolt_ is _5_ . For this reason, the mental power consumed _without_ expansion is 5 ÷ 2, rounded up to _3_ . Therefore, for a _doubled_ expansion, the amount of mental power consumed would be 3 x 2 = **6** . You would _not_ calculate (5 x 2) ÷ 2 = 5. ## **5.1.7.1 Expansion Types** Magic expansion includes the following types. ## 《 **Targets Expansion** 》 If you expand a spell that affects one enemy (or ally), it can instead affect multiple at the same time. If it's _two_ or _three_ targets...... the mental power consumed will also be _doubled_ or _tripled_ ....... In this case, you must roll the dice separately for each target to determine if they resist the magic, and other effects such as damage must also be determined separately. For spells that can perform this expansion, **targets** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. ## 《 **Area Expansion** 》 If you expand a spell that affects a specific area, it can affect a larger area than originally intended. If the area is _doubled_ or _tripled_ ...... the mental power consumed will also be _doubled_ or _tripled_ ....... You can also expand an area by a _fraction_ (x1.5, x8/3, etc.), in which case the fraction is _rounded up_ when determining the multiplier for mental power consumed. So for expansions such as _x 1.5_ and _x 8/3_ , the cost would be _double_ and _triple_ , respectively. Since it’s the area that gets expanded, the radius of a circle, a side of a square, etc., can only be increased by the _square root_ of the expansion rate. Please see Table 5-1: Area and Size Expansion. For spells that can perform this expansion, **area** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. ## 《 **Volume Expansion** 》 Some spells that affect a space or object can have their volume expanded. If the volume is expanded by _two_ or _three_ times...... the mental power consumed will also be _doubled_ or _tripled_ ....... Rounding should be handled the same way you would for area expansion. Since it’s the volume that gets expanded, the radius of a sphere, a side of a cube, etc., can only be increased by the _cube root_ of the expansion rate. Please see Table 5-1: Area and Size Expansion. For spells that can perform this expansion, **target volume** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. **Table 5-1: Area and Size Expansion** |Expansion
Rate|Area Expansion
Length|Volume Expansion
Length| |---|---|---| |x 2|1.4|1.3| |x 3|1.7|1.4| |x 4|2|1.6| |x 5|2.2|1.7| |x 6|2.4|1.8| |x 7|2.6|1.9| |x 8|2.8|2.0| |x 9|3.0|2.1| |x 10|3.2|2.2| Note: On the table, _expansion rate_ is the multiplier for mental power consumed. When you cast a spell by consuming that multiplied amount of mental power, multiply the number in _area expansion length_ or _volume expansion length_ by the number (radius, side) that represents the spell’s _area of effect_ , and the result will be the spell’s _area_ . For spells cast on a flat surface, use the number in the _area expansion length_ column, and for spells cast into space, use the number in the _volume expansion length_ column. For example, when you cast the ancient magic _Light_ , whose area is _A space with a 10 meter radius_ , by consuming _double_ the mental power, the resulting area will be a spherical space with a radius of _10 x 1.3 =_ _**13 meters**_ . ## 《 **Duration Expansion** 》 For spells whose effects last a certain amount of time, you can expand that duration. If the duration is _doubled_ or _tripled_ ...... the mental power consumed will also be _doubled_ or _tripled_ ....... You can also increase it by a fraction, such as x1.5, in which case the fraction is _rounded up_ when determining the multiplier for mental power consumed. For spells that can perform this expansion, **duration** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. ## 《 **Distance Expansion** 》 Magic basically only works up to a certain distance. However, there are spells that can extend their reach. If reach distance is _doubled_ or _tripled_ ...... the mental power consumed will also be _doubled_ or _tripled_ ....... You can also increase it by a fraction, such as x1.5, in which case the fraction is _rounded up_ when determining the multiplier for mental power consumed. 61

# 5.1.8 Magic Contraction

For spells that can perform this expansion, **distance** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. 

## 《 **Damage Expansion** 》 

For spells that deal damage, expansion can increase the certainty of that damage. If you _double_ or _triple_ the amount of mental power you consume...... you can make a damage check (strike roll) _twice_ or _three times_ . The damage you will deal is equal to the _greatest_ of all the strike rolls you made in this way ( _not_ the total). 

For spells that can perform this expansion, **damage certainty** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. 

## 《 **Final Score Increase** 》 

There's some magic whose effects are negated or reduced if they're resisted by the target, but will also let you _increase_ the magic's final score (resist target score) in order to prevent this. This increase in final score may also take effect if a nullifying spell is cast later. 

If you _double_ or _triple_ the amount of mental power you consume...... you can add a _+1_ or _+2_ bonus to your final score. 

For spells that can perform this expansion, **final score** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. 

This expansion requires a deep proficiency in magic. Therefore, it cannot be done infinitely. Final score can only be increased up to a maximum of _caster's rune master skill level ÷ 2 (rounded up)_ . That’s up to _+1 (double)_ for 1st~2nd level, _+2 (triple)_ for 3rd~4th level, and _+5 (sextuple)_ for 9th~10th level. 

## 《 **Effect Expansion** 》 

Some expansions don’t fall into any of the above types. For spells that can perform these special expansions, **effect** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details, and an explanation is given under each spell. 

## **5.1.7.2 Combined Expansion** 

Some magic allows for multiple expansion types. In these cases, you can perform multiple expansions at the same time. However, at this time, the amount of mental power consumed will become extremely large. By multiplying the rates determined for each expansion, you can determine the total multiplied rate of mental power consumed. 

## **Example:** 

Suppose a sorcerer casts an _Energy Bolt_ on three enemies ( _x 3_ expansion) with a +1 to his final score ( _x 2_ expansion) and he makes 4 damage checks for each ( _x 4_ expansion). In this case, the multiplied rate of mental power consumed is 3 x 2 x 4 = **24!** 

## **5.1.8** 

## **Magic Contraction** 

Magic can also be **contracted** as opposed to expanded. However, this does _not_ reduce the amount of mental power consumed. 

## **5.1.8.1 Contraction Types** 

Magic contractions include the following: 

## 《 **Area Contraction** 》 

You can make the area of effect narrower than originally intended. The shape (usually a circle) _cannot_ be changed. You also cannot cast it in such a way that it hits the enemy directly in the eyes. 

## 《 **Volume Contraction** 》 

Similar to area contraction, you can also reduce the volume of the targeted object or space. Again, the shape cannot be changed, and you cannot directly aim it at the enemy's eyes. 

## 《 **Duration Contraction** 》 

Magic with a duration can be optionally shortened. However, you _cannot_ set a time that causes the effect to wear off in the middle of a round. You may _only_ set it to wear off at the _end_ of a round. You must also decide how long you want the magic to last _before_ you cast it. Magic that’s already been cast cannot be canceled at will. 

## 《 **Magic Power Decrease** 》 

Magic power can be optionally reduced. This will reduce the final score and, if it's damage magic, _also_ reduce bonus damage (it’s not possible to _only_ reduce the final score or _only_ reduce bonus damage). You must declare how many points you’ll reduce _before_ you roll the dice. If used on multiple targets, you must reduce it by the same amount for _all_ of them. Also, if the side casting the magic rolls double sixes, the resisting side will _also_ need double sixes, no matter _how much_ magic power was declared to be decreased (if it's a monster, it _cannot_ resist). 

## 《 **Effect Contraction** 》 

All magic that can expand their effects can also contract their effects. 

## **Combined Expansion 5.1.8.2 and Contraction** 

Although it's not often done, it's possible to perform magic expansion _and_ contraction at the same time. For example, you could _extend_ the area of effect while _reducing_ magic power. The mental power required at this time is decided by _expansion_ . It _cannot_ be reduced by contraction. For example, if you _double_ the area _and_ reduce magic power by _-4_ , the mental power consumed will still be _double_ . 

62

# 5.1.9 Magic Types

## **5.1.9** 

## **Magic Types** 

There’s several kinds of magic that share the same characteristics. 

These characteristics are presented here as **magic types** . 

## 《 **Damage** 》 

A spell that deals damage to the target's life force or mental power. Spells of the damage type always have a clearly defined _strike power_ . If you manage to break through the target's resist, use the spell's strike power to determine damage by making a _strike roll_ using the Rating Table, same as if you had hit with a weapon in weapon combat. The key number is, of course, the indicated _strike power_ . If you roll 10 or greater during the strike roll, a _critical_ will occur and deal even more damage, same as in weapon combat. Conversely, If you roll double ones, it does _not_ deal any damage at all. 

Most magic of the **damage** type can still deal damage even if it's resisted by the target, only with a reduced effect. In this case, you must make the strike roll after _subtracting 10_ from strike power. Also, at this time, no matter how high you roll during your strike roll, a _critical_ will _not_ occur. 

In either case, the caster's magic power can be added as bonus damage to the result of the strike roll. Also, the target's adventurer level (or the monster's monster level) is subtracted from the damage. However, there’s an _exception_ if you roll _double ones_ during your strike roll. In this case, no matter how much magic power your character (monster) has, they _cannot_ deal damage to the target at all. 

Also, even magic of the _damage_ type may in rare cases take no effect if resisted. At this time, if the target successfully resists, you _cannot_ make a strike roll. 

## 《 **Target Score** 》 

Some magic can nullify the effects of other magic. For example, the ancient magic _Dispel Magic_ nullifies the effects of most magic. 

Most magic is automatically nullified if a nullifying spell is used, but there are some that are not. This is magic that has a _target score_ . 

When casting any magic, no matter what kind of magic it is, you must roll 2D to check whether an automatic failure (double ones) will occur or not. If you're trying to nullify magic that has a _target score_ with another spell, you must use that other spell's 2D roll to determine your final score. This final score equals _magic power + 2D roll_ (similar to a final score for other success rolls). 

If this final score is greater than or equal to the _target score_ originally set for that first magic that was cast, then that first magic can be nullified. 

This _target score_ equals the final score of the first magic that was cast (in other words, the magic you're about to nullify). Therefore, when an adventurer uses magic that has a _target score_ , that player should note down that score and remember it. However, if you feel this is cumbersome, or if you forget to make a note, then when it becomes necessary (nullifying magic has been used), you can roll 2D on the spot to determine a final score = _target score_ . 

When you use magic that nullifies other magic, if your roll is _double sixes_ , the magic will _automatically_ be nullified, no matter what the _target score_ is. 

Conversely, if the dice roll double sixes when determining the _target score_ (the success score of the first magic), you _must_ roll another double sixes in order to nullify that magic. 

## 《 **Counters (Negates)** 》 

When magic is cast against other magic, their effects may completely cancel each other out. For example, there are two kinds of ancient magic, _Decrease Weight_ (which makes an object lighter) 

and _Increase Weight_ (which makes an object heavier), but when both of these are cast on the same object at the same time, they will cancel each other out and disappear without taking effect. 

Such magic, that is, magic that negates the effect of other magic, but at the same time is negated by that other magic without taking its own effect, is called **counters (negates):** type. 

When casting _counters (negates):_ magic to cancel the effect of certain magic, if the magic that was cast prior has a _target score_ , then according to the definition of _magic with a target score_ , the magic that's cast after _cannot_ cancel the prior magic unless its final score is greater than or equal to the determined _target score_ . 

## 《 **Counters (Overpowers)** 》 

When certain magic is cast against other magic, it can cancel the effect of the prior magic but still take its own effect afterwards. This is magic of the **counters (overpowers):** type. 

For example, the ancient magic _Darkness_ can nullify the effects of the spirit magic _Will-O-Wisp_ and still close the surrounding area in darkness, which means _Darkness_ itself is more effective. 

However, this is often not automatic. One, is when the target _resists_ . In the case of magic that does not take effect if resisted, if the target _does_ resist it, the magic will _not_ take its effect, and the effect of the _prior_ magic will continue. 

The other, is when the prior magic has a _target score_ . In this case, according to the definition of _magic with a target score_ , the magic cast after will _not_ take effect unless its final score is greater than or equal to the determined _target score_ . 

If the magic cast prior has a _target score_ and the magic cast after is _resistible_ , please follow the procedure below to make a check. 

① First, confirm the _target score (= final score)_ of the magic cast prior (if you forgot, determine it now). 

63

② Next, the one casting the magic must roll 2D to find their final score (by adding their magic power). 

③ Compare _target score in_ ① with _final score in_ ②. If the final score does not reach the target score, the magic will _not_ be cast. This ends here. 

④ If _final score in_ ② is greater than or equal to _target score in_ ①, the target may attempt to _resist_ . The _resist target score_ equals the _final score in_ ②. 

⑤ If the target _successfully resisted_ in ④, the new magic cast will _not_ take effect. The effect of the _prior_ magic will continue. If the target _failed to resist_ , the new magic cast _will_ take effect and the effect of the prior magic will be _negated_ . 

_Counters (overpowers):_ magic does not mean that certain magic is definitely stronger than other magic. It's possible for magic A to _counter (overpower)_ magic B, and at the same time for magic B to _counter (overpower)_ magic A. 

Also, all magic _counters (overpowers)_ itself. Therefore, if certain magic takes effect, and you cast that same magic on the same target _multiple_ times, the effect will _not_ be cumulative. 

## 《 **Mind** 》 

This is magic that affects the target's mental state. This kind of magic can only take effect on one target at a time. If magic of the **mind** type is cast on a target, and other magic also of the _mind_ type is cast on that target, the _new_ magic will take effect, and the prior magic will be _nullified_ . 

In other words, all _mind_ magic _counters (overpowers)_ other _mind_ magic. Note that many are _resistible_ magic, and some have _target scores_ . 

## 《 **Physical Ability** 》 

This is magic that affects the target’s physical abilities (strength, etc.). Magic of this type can _only_ take effect on _humans_ , _fae_ , and _youma_ . 

Also, with magic of the **physical ability** type, two or more cannot affect 

the same target at the same time. Please resolve them as _counters (overpowers)_ magic. 

It’s possible for both _mind_ and _physical ability_ magic to each affect the same target separately. 

## 《 **Weapon Enhancement** 》 

Magic that targets a weapon and enhances it. Two or more of this kind of magic cannot affect the same weapon at the same time. Please resolve them as _counters (overpowers)_ magic. 

Weapons that have been given power by ancient magic, silver weapons, or mithril silver weapons are assumed to already have _non-removable_ (see below) **weapon enhancement** magic cast on them. Therefore, that power _cannot_ be nullified, so even if you cast new _weapon enhancement_ magic, it’ll be _useless_ . 

## 《 **Non-removable** 》 

Some magic, such as _Dispel Magic_ (ancient magic) and _Remove Curse_ (holy magic), can be used as counter magic that _removes_ other magic. However, magic of the **non-removable** type _cannot_ be removed by other magic. 

Although, the ancient magic _Perfect Cancellation_ **is** able to remove _nonremovable_ magic. Also, sometimes there is counter magic that counters _certain_ magic, which if specified, will be removed by _that_ counter magic. It's also possible to remove _non-removable magic_ by directly using the power of a god (wicked god) with the holy/dark magic _Call God_ . 

## 《 **Curse** 》 

_Dispel Magic_ (ancient magic) can be used to remove most magic, but for magic of the **curse** type, this is not possible. However, _Remove Curse_ (holy magic) **can** be used to remove it. 

## 《 **Detection** 》 

A spell that detects objects, people, magic power, etc. These can be targeted by the ancient magic _Counter Sense_ . These spells also ignore the protection of the ancient magic _Magic Protection_ . 

## 《 **Focus** 》 

Magic has a fixed _duration_ . Magic of the **focus** type is that in which the magic's effect lasts as long as the caster (the one who casts the magic) is _focused_ . While focusing, the caster cannot take the following actions: 

Use other magic 

Attack another with a weapon (or barehanded) 

Move more than 3 meters in one round 

Speak words 

Any other voluntary action 

Even while focusing, if another attacks you with a weapon, etc. you can still attempt to _evade_ it. If magic is cast on you, it's also possible to _resist_ it. 

Also, when the target is no longer within the magic's area of effect (out of reach, out of sight, etc.), the magic will _lose_ its effect, even if you continue to focus. 

If the caster suffers damage to their life force or mental power, their focus will be interrupted and the magic's effect will be _lost_ . 

If the caster wishes to voluntarily release their focus (in order to take another action), they must declare it at the _beginning_ of the round, when no one else has made their action announcements yet. If your focus is interrupted due to damage, etc. your focus will be interrupted at the timing in which the damage is dealt. 

## 《 **Illusion** 》 

A spell that deceives the five senses of the target. These spells deceive the 

64

# 5.1.10 How to Read Magic Details

opponent’s sensory organs and give them false sensations. There are three types of senses through which monsters perceive their surroundings: **five senses** , **pseudo** senses, and **magic** senses. Magic of the **illusion** type is effective on those that perceive their surroundings with _five senses_ or _pseudo_ senses. Humans and fae perceive their surroundings through _five senses_ , so they can all be targeted by _illusions_ . 

If the target does not have the senses to deceive, this spell will have no effect at all. It is completely ineffective against monsters who perceive their surroundings through _magic_ senses, and illusions that deceive vision are useless against monsters who _lack_ vision. 

The effects of spells of the _illusion_ type can be detected by a resistance roll. However, in order to do this, you must first be suspicious of the illusion. The game master should decide in which cases it seems suspicious. In the case of player characters (adventurers), this may be when the players themselves suspect an illusion. For NPCs and monsters, the game master should decide based on their intelligence. 

For example, suppose a fake floor is created above a pit using a _Create Image_ (ancient magic) spell. No matter how smart you are, you’re not likely to notice this. But what if whoever was walking at the front of the line had the misfortune to step on top of it and fall? 

No matter how foolish you are, you should suspect that the floor is an illusion. 

Or, what if you were in a hallway with a thick layer of dust, but only one part of it was free of dust? 

It might be unreasonable for a monster with low intelligence, such as a goblin, but a human of average intelligence would find it suspicious. 

In the event of such a suspicion, the target of the illusion can make a mental power resistance roll. The target score is the final score from when the illusion spell was cast (if you forgot, roll the dice and determine it again). If you’re 

successful, you’ll be able to see through the illusion and know the correct situation. However, you can only make this resistance roll _once_ . Once you fail, no matter how suspicious it may be, you won't be able to detect its true form (of course, you _can_ walk around suspicious floors). 

## 《 **Ritual** 》 

Magic of the **ritual** type can increase its effect (final score) due to carefully preparing and performing a ritual. 

With these spells, you can add +1 to your final score for each day you perform the ritual. If additional casters who can use the same spell also participate in the ritual, you will gain +1 to your final score for each one. Even humans who cannot use spells can participate in the ritual. In this case, you can gain +1 to your final score for every 10 people (rounded down). However, the ritual procedures are very complicated, so those who have not been taught the procedure in advance will not be able to participate in the ritual. If it's ancient magic, the caster must have been taught at a school such as an _academy of sages_ , etc. and if it's holy (dark) magic, they must be a believer. 

These bonuses are stacked in the form of _days x participants_ . For example, suppose a priest performs a _Remove Curse_ ritual. If the ritual takes two days (+2), one extra priest who can also use _Remove Curse_ participates (+1), and 30 believers participate (+3), the final score bonus will be _2 x (1 + 3) =_ _**+8**_ . _Fractions_ of the number of days must be _rounded down_ , so if you can't spend a _full_ day, the final score bonus will be _0_ , regardless of how many participants there are. 

Unlike magic expansion, there is _no_ limit to the increase in final score through rituals. 

A ritual must be performed without sleep or rest, and is usually limited to 2 days. If someone drops out during the ritual, their number will not be considered at all. In the previous example, if 10 believers drop out, even if it's right after the ritual begins, just 

before the ritual ends, or at any timing in between, the final score bonus will be _2 x (1 + 2) =_ _**+6**_ . 

The person who makes the success roll is the one who takes the center role in performing the ritual. 

The game master may also use a ritual to perform other expansions (targets, area, volume, etc.) that the magic would allow. 

## 《 **Lost** 》 

Among ancient magic, there are some spells known only to a limited number of sorcerers, and spells that were lost with the fall of the ancient kingdom. These are called **lost spells** . 

The game master gets to determine which spells are considered lost spells. It's acceptable to have _all_ spells on the magic list be lost, or conversely, to have all magic be _known_ and _no_ spells be lost. 

If a spell says _lost_ under its magic details, it may be easier to maintain balance when enjoying the game if you consider it a lost spell. Unless the game master has a particular reason, please consider these to be lost spells. 

## **5.1.10** 

## **How to Read Magic Details** 

Magic details are each written in the following format: 

## **[Sample Magic Details]** 

Base Mental Power Cost=0 

Distance=Caster 

Area=Caster 

Duration=Until you forget the contents Effect=Understand how to read magic details Type=Non-removable Expansion=Resist=None 

This spell allows the caster to understand how to read magic details. 

65

heading_path: 5.1.11 Ancient Magic and the Sorcerer Skill
text:
# 5.1.11 Ancient Magic and the Sorcerer Skill

## 《 **Base Mental Power Cost** 》 

The base mental power cost represents how much mental power is consumed when using that magic. As previously explained, this base mental power cost divided by the corresponding rune master skill level equals the actual mental power consumed by the caster. 

## 《 **Distance** 》 

This represents the distance the magic can reach. Basically, magic works on things that the caster can see and are within this distance. Any exceptions will always be noted in the explanation that follows. If the distance is **touch** , the caster must touch the target with their hand or (for ancient magic) with their magical catalyst (see below). To cast these spells in combat, you must be _engaged_ with the target. At this time, there is no need to roll dice to determine whether you've touched your opponent, like with an attack hit check (you can touch them automatically). 

## 《 **Area** 》 

This represents the target that the magic affects. 

If it says **caster** , the magic works only on the caster who cast the spell. 

When it says **one** , it works on one enemy or ally. It is also possible for casters to cast it on themselves. 

If it works on a **specific space or area** , then everything within that area is targeted. For example, a _Fireball_ explosion deals explosion damage to everything within a 3 meter radius. It makes no difference whether it's the caster's enemies, allies, or the caster. There are many other ways to write this, but it should be clear if you read the description. 

## 《 **Duration** 》 

This represents how long the magic will remain in effect. This includes **instant** , **18 rounds** , **1 day** , **permanent** , **until focus ends** or **until certain** 

**conditions are met** . For magic whose duration is _instant_ , the magic itself disappears in an instant, but its effects remain. Wounds caused by a _Fireball_ explosion will remain, and if you receive healing by _Cure Wounds_ , those wounds will be recovered instantly. 

## 《 **Effect** 》 

This is a brief description of the magic's effect. Once you've read the detailed description, you'll be able to recall the general effect of the magic just by looking at this. 

## 《 **Type** 》 

Represents the type of magic. Please see 5.1.9: Magic Types. **Counters (negates):** and **counters (overpowers):** describe magic that can be negated _by_ this magic, while **countered (negated) by:** and **countered (overpowered) by:** describe magic that can negate _this_ magic. However, _Dispel Magic_ , _Counter Sense_ , _Suppress Elemental_ , _Dispel Order_ , _Rune Shield_ , _Perfect Cancellation_ , _Neutralize Elemental_ , and _Remove Curse_ omit this. Magic of the **mind** , **physical ability** , and **weapon enhancement** types also omit _counters_ and _countered by_ , since they are sufficiently described. 

## 《 **Expansion** 》 

Represents which expansion types can be performed. Please see 5.1.7.1: Expansion Types. 

## 《 **Resist** 》 

This represents whether the magic can be resisted, and what will happen if it is resisted. 

**None** : No resist can be performed. Spells always take their effect. 

**Negated Effect** : If resisted, the spell does not take its effect. 

**Reduced Effect** : If resisted, the spell only takes an incomplete effect. Spells of the _damage_ type will have their strike 

power reduced by -10 and can no longer critical. 

After these details are briefly written, a detailed description of each spell is written. Be sure to read it over before attempting to use something you've never used before. 

## **5.1.11** 

## **Ancient Magic and the Sorcerer Skill** 

_High ancient_ are the great runes that were developed and studied during the time of the ancient kingdom. The power of these runes releases magic forces and brings about various effects. Many of the spells and runes of this system were lost with the fall of the ancient kingdom, but a small amount of them have been passed down to modern Forcelia, and are now referred to as **ancient magic** . The skill for using ancient magic is called the _sorcerer skill_ , and those who have this skill are called _sorcerers_ . 

## **Restrictions on Using 5.1.11.1 Ancient Magic** 

Ancient magic has powerful effects because it draws its source of power (which is sometimes called _mana_ ) directly from matter, but it is subject to the following two restrictions in order to cast the spells correctly: 

1. Must have a staff (mage's staff) as a _magical catalyst_ 

2. Due to complex gestures required, armor is limited to _cloth_ or _soft leather_ 

If you don't obey these two things, you won't be able to use ancient magic. Other rules follow the basic rules of magic. 

66

## **Basic Abilities of the 5.1.11.2 Sorcerer Skill** 

The sorcerer skill includes the following basic abilities in addition to spells: 

## 《 **High Ancient** 》 

Sorcerers can speak, read, and write in high ancient. It seems that these runes were used in official texts and conversation in the ancient kingdom, but now much of it has been lost, except for the few that remain in spellbooks. 

## 《 **Low Ancient** 》 

This was an everyday language of the ancient kingdom, called **low ancient** , and was the language used by citizens and slaves. Sorcerers can speak, read and write in this language. Low ancient was used in informal texts in the ancient kingdom. 

## **Characteristics of Sorcerers 5.1.11.3 and Ancient Magic** 

Sorcerers and the ancient magic they handle have the following characteristics: 

## 《 **Spellbook** 》 

A sorcerer must somehow preserve the ancient magic spells they've learned. Otherwise, even those with the greatest minds cannot possibly memorize all the words, actions, etc. needed to cast spells. 

For this reason, it is customary for sorcerers to write these spells down in a book called a **spellbook** . A spellbook is, so to speak, just a handwritten notebook, and the spellbook itself has no magical power of its own. However, because it's a very important thing, it would be a disaster if it were lost or stolen, so some sorcerers may keep several copies in reserve. 

When you go on an adventure, or you're in a situation where you'd need to use magic, you'd usually bring your spellbook with you. However, no sorcerer looks at their spellbook every 

time they try to cast a spell. This is because the words and actions of a spell are usually sharply memorized in your head. A spellbook is used to review them, always confirming and ensuring your memory. 

There are no special restrictions on spellbooks according to the rules. Nevertheless, keep in mind that sorcerers in Sword World always keep their spellbooks with them, and remember to take very good care of them. 

## 《 **Magical Catalyst** 》 

Sorcerers require a special medium to cast spells. This is a **magical catalyst** , and it is through this that a sorcerer exercises the power of a spell. Usually, a specially-made mage's staff is used as a magical catalyst, made from a wooden staff with a _Create Device_ spell cast on it. This is why you must have a mage's staff as a restriction when using ancient magic. However, some sorcerers substitute other things besides staves. For example, Laverna, the witch of Orphan, uses an amethyst ring she wears on her right middle finger as her magical catalyst, and Cyanos, leader of the magic knights of Ramliearth, carries a small twig wand in his left hand as a magical catalyst.

Even spells with a _resist_ of _none_ can be resisted by _Magic Protection_ . In this case, the spell will take no effect at all. ## **[ Familiar ]** 

Base Mental Power Cost=15 

Distance=1 kilometer 

Area=1 specific animal 

Duration=Permanent 

Effect=You can turn a small animal into your familiar 

Type=Target score, Non-removable Expansion=Final score Resist=None 

This spell has the effect of allowing a sorcerer to summon and control an animal to become their familiar. The animals that can become familiars are somewhat limited. For example, frogs or crows. Cats and owls are also famous familiars, and these animals will appear in response to the caster's summons and serve the caster. A sorcerer can have only one familiar. If a sorcerer who already has a familiar tries to summon a second one, it’ll show no effect whatsoever. 

This spell must always be performed as a ritual. The caster requires 3 full days of preparation, followed by 12 hours of spell chanting. Please refer to Table 5-2: Familiars to see what kind of animal will appear as a familiar in response to a summon. 

The summoned familiar and the sorcerer are always in mental contact, so they can communicate in a way similar to telepathy and can also share sensory organs. The caster can use their familiar's sensory organs to see and hear, and vice versa. Also, when you give a command to your familiar, the familiar will try to execute it, but there are natural limits to what it can actually do depending on the familiar's physical abilities. Abilities of each familiar are listed on the table, but you can find out more details by referring to an animal encyclopedia. 

The caster can also borrow mental points from their familiar to cast spells. In this case, the mental power that would've been consumed by the caster will instead be reduced from the familiar's mental points. Of course, you cannot make a substitution that would cause mental points to become negative, and a familiar whose mental points reach 0 will lose consciousness.

|Owl|4|4|Sees well in the dark. Can fy|
|Black cat|5|5|Sees well in the dark|

However, the number of points lost cannot go beyond the familiar's maximum life points or mental points. This spell has the effect of completely concealing the caster’s presence from others. In other words, not only will they become invisible, you will no longer be able to hear sounds made by the caster, and of course you won't be able to smell them. However, you can feel their presence if you touch them. This spell cannot be cast on others. The duration lasts as long as your focus continues. If you take any action that breaks your focus, the effect of this spell vanishes immediately. 

## **[ Shape Change ]** 

Base Mental Power Cost=20 Distance=Caster Area=Caster 

Duration=Depends on caster's intention Effect=Change the caster’s appearance Type=Target score Expansion=Final score Resist=None 

This is a spell that allows the caster to change their form into another living creature at will. However, the creatures they can change into are limited to about _10 times_ or _1/10th_ of the caster's weight. You can think of the maximum as a 6- meter tall titan and the minimum as a medium-sized cat. The caster can cancel the spell's effect at any time, but once it's canceled, you cannot take on that form again unless you cast the spell again. You can only change into humans, fae, youma, titans, and animals, and only those the caster knows in detail (you must succeed on a roll such as the sage skill's _monster check_ ). You can never change into something whose origins are not clearly known, such as magical beasts, mythical beasts, etc. 

Only the caster's body will change shape, the clothes and accessories they're wearing do not change. They will fall off or break in the process of changing. Also, after changing form, the caster's physical abilities will become the same as the 

creature they changed into. You will also gain special abilities such as flying. The caster will not lose their sense of self, and can cancel the spell at will. 

## **[ Sense Aura ]** 

Base Mental Power Cost=12 

Distance=Caster 

Area=Within the caster's view Duration=18 rounds 

Effect=The caster can distinguish the kind of powers present in their surroundings Type=Detection Expansion=Duration Resist=None 

By casting this spell, the caster can recognize the following powers at work in their surroundings: 

Spirit Power: This gives the caster the same effect that a shaman has with its basic ability. 

Magic Power: This allows you to know the presence of objects and spaces where ancient or holy magic is at work.

This spell only allows you to speak _one_ language each time you cast it, so in order to be able to speak _two_ or _three_ unknown languages at the same time, you must _expand_ the effect of the spell. Also, this spell only allows you to use languages used by intelligent creatures listed on Table 12-1: Languages (see p. 159), so that means you will not be able to speak to animals, etc.

## Expansion=Final score, distance Resist=Negated effect 

This is a spell that completely seals your opponent's movements. Those who fail to resist will lose their freedom and immediately become rigid on the spot. A target caught by this magic in an unstable state will likely fall down. If it were a bird, it would fall out of the sky. 

To maintain this spell, the caster must keep focus. Someone who's had this spell cast on them may only attempt to resist the first time the spell is cast.

This is a spell of destruction that is forbidden by wise sorcerers. It creates an explosion centered on a point in space within a 3 meter radius, dealing fire and shock damage to everything within. Its strike power is 20 (10 with no criticals on a successful resist).

The caster must know the actual object being targeted by the spell well enough to specify it. Therefore, unspecified objects 

74

such as _a sword_ or _a goblin_ cannot be detected (it's not possible if it's _my favorite sword_ ).

This spell creates illusions that affect the senses of hearing and vision. The illusion can be anything within its area of effect, but it cannot cause any realistic effects. For example, you can create a wall of fire via illusion, but you won't feel any heat even if you approach it, and you won't burn to death even if you jump into it. However, the illusions created by this spell are so realistic that they cannot be discovered just by looking at them normally. The spell's effect continues even if you touch it. If an illusion of a 

stone wall is in a passage, you can't see beyond it, but you're completely free to pass through it (if you try). However, this result will not destroy the illusion of the stone wall. As long as the caster maintains focus, they can freely change the illusion, but if they lose focus, only static or simple repeating illusions can remain. The caster can freely cancel the spell's effect, but a _Dispel Magic_ spell is required if someone else attempts to remove it.

Resist=None 

This spell has the effect of negating any one of the four major spirit powers of earth, water, fire, or wind. However, only one kind of spirit power can be erased at a time. This spell cannot be expanded to block two or more kinds of spirit power from working at the same time. As a result of this, spirit magic that depends on that spirit power will no longer be usable. Also, if a spell effect is present due to the negated spirit power (either gnome, undine, salamander, or sylph) within the spell's area of effect, it will be negated. If that spirit is present, it will also disappear from existence and return to the spirit world. However, this has no effect on greater spirits (behemoth, kraken, efreet, djinn). 

## **[ Skeleton Warrior ]**

A skeleton warrior, also known as a dragontooth warrior, is a kind of golem created from a dragon's tooth, and resembles a human skeleton with a sword and shield. This spell is what creates that formidable warrior. 

The caster can give commands to the skeleton warrior in high ancient. Only the caster can give commands. They cannot understand overly complex commands, but they handle simple commands accurately. For example, commands such as _kill anyone who enters this room_ or _kill that man_ are very simple and can be carried out, but a command such as _go to the next village and kidnap the chief's daughter_ cannot be carried out. 

The dragon's tooth, which is the source of the skeleton warrior, is a very valuable item as one of the treasures of the ancient kingdom. You'd have to pay a whopping 5000 silver coins to purchase it. 

See p. 223 for skeleton warrior scores.

You can only know that if it's a lie, it's a lie, and if it's an exaggeration, it's an exaggeration.

When you use this spell on an object that had _Seal Enchantment_ cast on it, its effect cannot be inferred if your final score does not exceed the target score determined by _Seal Enchantment_ . Duration=Permanent

This spell gives the target a " _do not ___ " command and makes them obey it. If the person who had the spell cast on them tries to break this prohibition command, they will suffer severe pain all over their body. The pain is so severe that if you continue to suffer it for more than 10 minutes, you will become mentally abnormal. 

The caster can cancel this spell at will, but the spell will not be removed even if the caster dies. 

This spell's effect only works on those who have some degree of intelligence. At least, if you're not intelligent enough to use language, this spell cannot be cast on you. 

## **[ Create Undead ]** 

Base Mental Power Cost=24 Distance=Touch Area=One corpse Duration=Permanent 

Effect=Turns a corpse into undead Type=Non-removable, lost, ritual Expansion=Target volume Resist=None 

_Create Undead_ is a spell that creates undead monsters such as skeletons and zombies. To use this spell, of course, you'll need a corpse (usually a human corpse is used). Depending on the state of decomposition of the corpse, it will determine whether it'll become a skeleton or a zombie. If you use this spell on a mummified or severely decomposed corpse, the corpse will be completely skeletonized and a skeleton will be born. If it's still a corpse before that happens, then a zombie will be born. The caster can give commands to the skeletons and zombies created in this 

76

way. The commands must be extremely simple, at best they can be made to protect a specific location. Even walking alongside you as guards is too difficult for them. For details on human skeletons and zombies, see 13.2.9: Undead (see p. 214). 

If you wish, you can turn non-human animals, fae, youma, titans, mythical beasts, magical beasts, etc. into undead, but to cast this on something larger than human size, you must consume additional mental power (points) to expand the effect. The required mental power (points) increases in proportion with the size of the target ( _double_ for an ogre, _seven times_ for a chimera, and over _1000 times_ for a 20-meter long dragon!). On the other hand, turning small animals into undead does not mean that they consume less mental power. 

Details for non-human skeletons and zombies should be decided by the game master. A rough guideline is to set life scores to 1/2 for skeletons, and 3/2 for zombies. Other scores may not need to be changed.

This is a spell that creates a mage's staff, which is a magical catalyst necessary for sorcerers to use ancient magic. All you need is a wooden staff of an appropriate length, and by casting this spell on that staff, you'll create a mage's staff. You can also use this spell to create a _magical catalyst_ other than a mage's staff.

Expansion=Resist=None 

To cast this spell, you need a corpse within a few days of death, so the corpse can be revived as a buau zombie (see p. 214). The caster can make the buau zombie obey commands. This spell only works on human, fae or youma corpses.

This is a clairvoyance spell, so to speak. By casting this spell, the caster can see things on the other side of obstacles such as walls, etc. as well as the interior of boxes, etc. As long as it's within the area of effect, the caster can adjust the desired range and distance they want to see at will. The clairvoyant information appears directly in your mind as an image, so you can still see even if the target location is closed in darkness.

Sometimes there are items from the ancient kingdom period that have this 

cast on them. Many of them are protected by _Seal Enchantment_ with a final score as high as 30 or greater. 

This spell has no effect when using the sage skill to appraise an item (however, if you don't think it has magical power, you might not try to appraise it). This spell cannot be cast on living things.

If you expand the effect and steal mental power (points) from multiple targets, you can recover the total mental power (points) you stole. order and movement speed reduced by half due to changes in their agility, and their evasion speed (points) will also take a penalty. _Full Potential_ is a spell that enhances the effects of _Physical Enchantment_ and raises potential capabilities of the human body to maximum. The result is the same effect as casting the _Sharpness_ , _Quickness_ and _Strength_ spells of _Physical Enchantment_ all at the same time. In other words, _dexterity_ , _agility_ , and _strength_ all increase by _6_ at the same time. ## **[ Magic Reflection ]**

79

This spell reflects magic cast on the caster directly back onto their opponent. If magic is cast on the person who casts this spell, that magic will rebound back to the opponent who cast it. At the same time, the effect of _Magic Reflection_ will disappear. 

If your magic has been reflected, you must resist against your own magic yourself. 

However, this can only reflect magic which has an area of effect of _one_ . In the case of magic that affects a space, _Magic Reflection_ has no effect.
