No| *Holy prayer and daemon scream are basically the same thing. However, there are slight differences depending on the god (wicked god) you believe in. **Now (believed to be) lost. ## **12.1.5** ## **Monster Language Ability** Some intelligent monsters have the ability to speak and read using a certain language. Of course, there are monsters with multiple language abilities. The table below lists the languages used by monsters. Monster details also have an entry called _Language_ . **[ Common ]** Soldier (knight, knight leader) ## **[ Regional ]** Pooka, featherfolk, goblin (shaman, lord), marshman, dark elf (all), spriggan, satyr, harpy (deela, deela king), medusa, androscorpio, scylla, twintail cat, lamia, sphinx, fungus, jack-o-lantern, dullahan, undead knight, imitator (thing), mutant monster, normal man, bandit (all), soldier (all), dark priest warrior (all), lycanthrope (all), redcap ## **[ Low Ancient ]** Twintail cat, telchines, head displacer, lamia, manticore, sphinx, buckbaird, humbaba, dragon (elder), wraith, mummy (master), vampire (undead king), homunculus (homunculus), shi, lesser daemon (all), doppelganger ## **[ Silent Spirit ]** Pixie, fairy, pooka, Featherfolk, merman (mermaid shaman), gremlin, goblin (shaman), dark elf (all), brownie, undine, salamander, sandman, sylph, sprite, gnome, frau, leprechaun, dryad, the spirit of life, valkyrie, giant (fire, frost), androscorpio, scylla, twintail cat, unicorn, mandrake, alraune **[ Elven ]** Dark elf (all) **[ Dwarven ]** Not among the monsters in this book **[ Imp ]** Imp, gremlin, marshman **[ Centaurian ]** Centaur **[ Goblin ]** Kobold, hobgoblin, goblin (all) **[ Giant ]** Yeti, ogre, spriggan, troll, hecatoncheir, giant (all), cyclops **[ Harpy ]** Featherfolk, harpy (all) **[ Fairy ]** Pixie, fairy, pooka, twintail cat **[ Merman ]** Merman (all), telchines **[ Minotaur ]** Minotaur **[ Lizardman ]** Lizardman (all), wyrm, dragon (all) ## **12.2** ## **Rules for Poison, Illness and Infection** In _Sword World RPG_ , illness is defined as a state in which the balance of spirit power within the body is disrupted. The concept of bacterial or viral contagion does not exist. There are two types of symptoms that can appear. One is due to **poison** and the other is due to **illness** . In addition, a wound may become **infected** , which is treated as an illness. Poisons force an imbalance in the spirit power within the body. As long as the poison remains in the body, even if the symptoms disappear, the balance of spirit power will be disrupted and the symptoms will appear again. However, if the poison itself is cleared away, the spirit power within the body will naturally return to a balanced state and restore health. On the other hand, illness is a state where the spirit power within the body remains unbalanced and in a fixed state. Think of it as the spirits inside your body that are under the illusion that a false equilibrium is the correct one. Illness infection occurs when spirits transmit information about the incorrect equilibrium state to spirits in other human bodies. The unknown spirit of life heals illness by showing other spirits in the body how to achieve balance. In _Sword World RPG_ , the phenomenon of a wound becoming infected means that _the container was broken and deformed, causing the contents to become unbalanced_ . The logic goes that the greater the wound, the greater the risk of infection. 159 ## **12.2.1 Poisons** This section explains the rules regarding poison. ## **Poison Treatment 12.2.1.1 and Detox Methods** Just like in our world, in Alecrast, using poison as a weapon is an immoral act. If a player character uses poison to defeat an enemy, they are sure to be treated as a coward and looked down upon by the public. Therefore, a proud warrior does _not_ want to use poison. On the other hand, assassins, evil rune masters, dark priests, etc. _do_ like to use poison. Poisons that have entered the body can be cleared using specific antidotes (if any), the holy magic _Cure Poison_ or _Refresh_ , or the spirit magic _Restore Health_ . To remove a poison with magic, the caster's final score (magic power + 2D roll) must be greater than or equal to the poison's **toxicity score** . Once a detox fails, the same caster cannot attempt detox magic until their magic power increases (and even if they try, they will always fail). Even if you detoxify someone _after_ they die from poison, they will _not_ come back to life. Some poisons have symptoms similar to illnesses, but they are _not_ natural illnesses and _cannot_ be cured with _Cure Disease_ . The spirit magic _Purification_ can make liquid poison harmless if the final score when casting the magic is greater than or equal to than the toxicity score. However, it’s _not_ possible to drive off a poison that’s _already_ entered the body. ## **12.2.1.2 Resistance to Poison** Poisons are not effective against everything. It has no effect on monsters that have no life such as undead, golems, etc. Some mythical and magical beasts have strong life force that makes them immune to poison. Some poison effects may or may not affect specific monsters. Monsters that use poison as weapons are immune to their _own_ poisons (but are still affected by _other_ poisons). ## **How to Read Poison 12.2.1.3 Details** Poison details are expressed as follows. **[ Ale (Dose) ]** Effect=Euphoria, drunkenness, in rare cases headache and vomiting, very rarely death Toxicity Score =10 Rarity=5 Effect Start Time=30 minutes later Effect Duration=2 days This is an alcoholic beverage made by fermenting barley. Drinking a little can help lighten your mood and relieve stress, but drinking too much can leave you drunk (in this state, you suffer a -4 penalty on all success rolls). You may also experience headaches and nausea after a certain period of time. Some adventurers, other than dwarves, have extremely low tolerance for alcohol, and such characters cannot resist this poison (?) no matter how high their adventurer level. It is up to the player to decide whether or not to set their character this way. ## 《 **Usage** 》 The **usage** of the poison is written in parentheses after the name. These are divided into **injected** , **inhaled** , **dose** , and **touch** . _Injected_ poisons are poisons that need to be delivered directly into the bloodstream, usually in liquid form. It's applied to small daggers and needles and used as traps on doors and treasure chests. It can be applied to knives and arrows to increase their killing power, but the poison usually wears off after about three uses and loses its effect, so it must be reapplied frequently. Many creatures, such as giant scorpions, poisonous snakes, etc. use injected poison as a weapon. This type of poison only takes effect if you lose 1 or more points of life force when you are hurt by a poisoned blade. Even if the blade hits, if it doesn't cause a wound, it has no effect. _Inhaled_ poisons are in gas or powder form and take effect when inhaled through the respiratory tract. This is also often used as a trap, but another method is to store it in a breakable bottle and use it by slamming it into your opponent. In rare cases, some creatures use poison gas as a weapon. If you’re trapped in a room filled with poison gas, you must make a resistance roll every round. If you hold your breath, you will not be affected by the gas, but of course you will not be able to speak or cast spells while holding your breath. An adventurer can only hold their breath for a number of rounds equal to their life force. _Dose_ poison is used by mixing it into food or drink and comes in powder or liquid form. It is mainly used for assassinations, so adventurers will barely use it. _Touch_ poison comes in liquid or powder form and can penetrate through the skin. There are not many types of them, and as expected they are often used as traps. ## 《 **Types of Poison** 》 Poisons are sometimes labeled as **magic** , **mind** , or **damage** , depending on how they affect you. Those that say _magic_ are poisons that have magical effects. These are basically magical items, so they respond to the ancient magic called _Sense Magic_ . Magical poisons and their effects can be removed with _Dispel Magic_ as well as _Purification_ , _Cure Poison_ , etc. Those that say _mind_ are poisons that affect the target's mind. The holy magic _Sanity_ is effective. The poison's effect also vanishes when another effect appears due to magic that belongs to _mind_ . Poisons that are both _magic_ and _mind_ are equivalent to magic that has a _target score_ . The _target score_ of a poison is the same as its _toxicity score_ (described later). 160 Those that say _damage_ are poisons that damage life force (points), but there are common rules for these types of poisons. Each poison has its own unique strike power and bonus damage. If this type of poison enters your body, you will suffer this damage. Make a strike roll as you would for any magic that deals damage. If you roll 10 or greater, it becomes a critical. Those affected by the poison can reduce this damage. For characters, the score is adventurer level, and for monsters, it’s monster level. After deducting this score, if 1 or more damage remains, your life force (points) will be subtracted by the amount of damage that remains. Those affected by the poison can attempt a life force resistance roll. If you successfully resist, the damage will be reduced (in this case, strike power will be -10 and no criticals will occur) or you will not suffer any damage at all. ## 《 **Effect** 》 A brief summary of the effects of the poison. ## 《 **Toxicity Score** 》 Each poison has a **toxicity score** . When the poison enters the body, make a life force resistance roll (for monsters, a life _point_ resistance roll) using the toxicity score as the target score. If you fail, the effect will appear, but if you succeed, the effect will only appear incomplete or won't appear at all. The toxicity scores listed here are just examples; by diluting the substance, the toxicity score can be made as low as desired, and by concentrating it, it can be made somewhat greater. When you cast the holy magic _Cure Poison_ on someone who has been poisoned, compare the final score when casting the magic to this toxicity score. If the final score is equal to or greater than the toxicity score, the poison can be removed by the magic. ## 《 **Rarity** 》 This represents how famous the poison is. The higher this score, the rarer the poison. An adventurer must make a success roll using _sage skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score, and if they succeed, they will know about the poison. You can also _guess_ the name of the poison when you see the symptoms of the poisoned person. If your success roll is 4 or more above the **rarity** , you can also know how to _make_ the poison and its antidote (if it has one). However, even if you know how to make it, whether or not you _can_ make it is another matter. Some poisons require very rare ingredients. If a player declares that they want to make a poison (or antidote), the game master is responsible for deciding which materials are needed. The time it takes to mix a poison (or antidote) varies from one hour to one week. Also, poisons with magical effects can _only_ be mixed by sorcerers. ## 《 **Effect Start Time** 》 This is the time it takes from when the poison enters the body until it begins to take effect. Some poisons have an immediate effect, while others take many hours to take effect. ## 《 **Effect Duration** 》 It represents the time from when the poison's effect appears until it disappears. ## 《 **Description** 》 After basic data is presented, a detailed description is provided. ## **12.2.1.4 Poison List** Typical poisons include the following. There are many other types of poisons as well. **[ Iocane (Dose)** Effect=Fatal Toxicity Score=15 Rarity=12 Effect Start Time= 1 round later Effect Duration=Instant This is the most terrifying poison used for assassination. This is because this poison is completely transparent, tasteless, and odorless, so it’ll never be noticed if it's mixed into alcohol or food. If you fail to resist, you will die, but if you succeed, there’ll be no harm at all. ## **[ Eternal Child (Dose) ]** Effect=Make the person who took it into a child Toxicity Score=14 Rarity=16 Effect Start Time=1 hour later Effect Duration=Permanent This is a sweet-smelling liquid that is mixed into sweets, etc. Those who fail to resist become young children. Their ability scores are all divided by 6 (rounded up). If you are detoxed, you will recover (grow) within a few days, but unless you are detoxed, you will have to act with the same physique and mentality as a child. This poison does _not_ have the effect of reversing a character’s age, and may cause them to die of old age while remaining a child. ## **[Kaleidoscope (Injected or Dose)]** Effect=Causes illusions Toxicity Score=11 Rarity=14 Effect Start Time=1 round later Effect Duration=1 hour This is a hallucinogenic poison. Those who fail to resist will be attacked by hallucinations starting on the next round. Enemies may appear to have multiple parts, allies may appear to be monsters, or the surrounding scenery may appear to have changed. The victim suffers a -4 penalty on all checks while this condition lasts. 161 **[Gremlin's Grin (Injected/Magic)]** Effect=Cause memory loss Toxicity Score=13 Rarity=13 Effect Start Time=1 round later Effect Duration=Permanent This is so named because its effect is similar to the _Forget_ magic used by gremlins. Those who fail to resist will have no memory of their past, and will be unable to use any abilities related to intelligence (those that use intelligence bonus to calculate a baseline score). ## **[Centauri Blood (Touch/Damage)]** Effect=Deals strike power 30 damage plus 12 bonus damage every hour. Resist reduces effect Toxicity Score=14 Rarity=14 Effect Start Time=1 hour later Effect Duration=Permanent A poison said to have been invented by a wise centaur. There is a myth that it’s made from the blood of centaurs, but it’s actually made from the fruit of certain plants. It’s used in assassinations by soaking it into items that are close to the body, such as underwear, etc. It's naturally a red liquid, but if you soak it in a cloth and then wash it, it becomes colorless. Even if you wash it, only the color will come off, and the poisonous ingredients will remain on the cloth. What's more, this poison is completely harmless at room temperature, and only becomes toxic when warmed by body heat. Those wearing cloth soaked with centauri blood will experience intense pain and heat after one hour. Once that happens, you can no longer strip it from your body or wash it off, and it will continue to deal damage every hour from then on. The damage is a terrifying strike power 30 plus bonus damage 12. A successful resistance roll will reduce the damage. This poison is said to have killed a famous hero. **[Sandman's Sand (Inhaled/Mind)]** Effect=Put to sleep Toxicity Score=10 Rarity=14 Effect Start Time=1 round later Effect Duration=1 hour It has this name because its effect is similar to the spirit magic _Sleep_ , but it’s also called _black lotus_ because it's made from black lotus seeds. The strong flower-scented gas will put anyone who fails to resist into a state of sleep. ## **[Stone Tongue (Injected)]** Effect=Become unable to speak Toxicity Score=12 Rarity=14 Effect Start Time= 1 round later Effect Duration=24 hours A neurogenic poison that paralyzes the speech center, rendering those who fail to resist unable to speak or cast spells for 24 hours. ## **[Zombie Maker (Dose/Mind)]** Effect=Paralyze the mind, making it into a robot Toxicity Score=11 Rarity=14 Effect Start Time=1 round later Effect Duration=24 hours This is a black liquid poison and is a type of narcotic. Those who fail to resist will lose all will and emotion and become like a doll. If someone pulls their arm, they will stagger, and if someone calls them, they will respond in an emotionless voice, but they cannot do anything of their own will. Around one additional hour of suggestions can transform the victim into a robot that follows commands. Victims turned into robots faithfully follow the commands of the person who gave them the suggestions, and will casually kill even their family and friends (they _cannot_ use magic). However, if you suffer a very strong physical or mental shock, you may recover from the paralysis even before the duration expires. **[Dark Blade (Injected/Damage)]** Effect=Become disabled, and suffer strike power 20 damage plus bonus damage 6 every 10 minutes. Resist negates effect Toxicity Score=15 Rarity=10 Effect Start Time= 1 round later Effect Duration=Permanent A poison often used by assassins, it is a pitch-black viscous liquid. If you are wounded by a knife coated with this poison, you will suffer damage every 10 minutes thereafter. The victim continues to suffer damage until the poison wears off or they die. During this time, you will be in terrible pain and unable to take any action. If treatment is delayed, you may remain in a coma for several days after the poison wears off. If you succeed on a resistance roll, you will not suffer any damage. **[Deathlike Sleep (Dose)]** Effect=Put into suspended animation Toxicity Score=14 Rarity=14 Effect Start Time= 1 round later Effect Duration=12 hours This is a liquid poison. Those who fail to resist will stop breathing and their heart will stop, making them appear completely dead. However, they’re actually in a state of suspended animation and will return to normal after 12 hours. **[Deadly Dust (Inhaled/Damage)]** Effect=Deals strike power 10 damage plus bonus damage 1. Resist negates effect. Toxicity Score=10 Rarity=9 Effect Start Time=Immediately Effect Duration=Instant A fine powdered poison that requires a resistance roll each time you inhale, resulting in damage if you fail. 162 ## **[ Dream Runner (Inhaled) ]** Effect=Gives happy dreams Toxicity Score=10 Rarity=10 Effect Start Time= 6 rounds later Effect Duration=1 hour This is smoke produced when the leaves of certain plants are burned. Those who fail to resist will fall asleep and have happy dreams. If you use it too many times, you may harm your health or go crazy. ## **[ Bad Joke (Inhaled)** Effect=Make you laugh Toxicity Score=11 Rarity=10 Effect Start Time= 1 round later Effect Duration=1 round This is a gas that smells good. Those who fail to resist will end up laughing, unable to cast spells that round, and will suffer a -2 penalty on all checks. ## **[Beast Maker (Dose/Magic)]** Effect=Turn into an animal Toxicity Score=13 Rarity=15 Effect Start Time= 12 rounds later Effect Duration=Permanent This is a liquid with a slight odor, used by mixing into cooking, etc. It has an effect similar to the ancient magic _Shape Change_ , and those who drink it and fail to resist will be transformed into an animal. There are several types of _Beast Maker_ , including ones that turn you into a pig, ones that turn you into a dog and ones that turn you into a horse. Particularly powerful ones can turn you into a mythical beast such as a scylla, etc. ## **[Black Death (Touch, Injected, or Inhaled/Magic, Damage)]** Effect=Turns skin black and deals strike power 20 damage plus 6 bonus damage every hour. Resist reduces effect Toxicity Score=13 Rarity=12 Effect Start Time=1 hour later Effect Duration=24 hours This is a special black viscous poison that enters the body when it comes in contact with the skin. It will continue to deal damage every hour thereafter. **[ Blue Nail (Injected)** Effect=Full body paralysis Toxicity Score=10 Rarity=10 Effect Start Time=1 round later Effect Duration=12 hours This is similar to the poison contained in ghoul claws, and those who fail to resist will be paralyzed all over, unable to move or speak. ## **[White Marble (Inhaled/Magic)]** Effect=Stiffens the whole body Toxicity Score=11 Rarity=13 Effect Start Time= 2 rounds later Effect Duration=Permanent It's a white gas. Those who inhale this and fail to resist will have their entire body stiffen and become like a statue. Victims retain their will and senses, but are unable to move a single finger. ## **[Midnight Scream (Touch/Mind)]** Effect=Cause intense fear Toxicity Score=13 Rarity=14 Effect Start Time=6 rounds later Effect Duration=1 hour This is a viscous poison and is used by smearing it on doorknobs, etc. Those who touch this poison and fail to resist will be struck with intense fear. Determine its effect using Table 5-5: Fears (see p. 89). ## **[Moonlight Drone (Dose/Magic)]** Effect=Change gender Toxicity Score=14 Rarity=15 Effect Start Time=1 round later Effect Duration=Permanent This is a small granular poison, and if someone drinks it and fails to resist, they will become a woman if they're a man, and a man if they're a woman. There are no other penalties. Additionally, there are no changes to the magic that shamans can use. If you drink it again, you can return to normal. ## **[Medusa Eyes (Dose/Magic)]** Effect=Petrification Toxicity Score=16 Rarity=13 Effect Start Time=6 rounds later Effect Duration=Permanent This is a clear liquid poison that has a slight odor, so it's used by mixing with alcohol, etc. Those who fail to resist will have their bodies petrified and become stone statues. ## **[ Love Passion (Inhaled, Dose/Magic, Mind) ]** Effect=Fall in love with the first person you see Toxicity Score=13 Rarity=11 Effect Start Time=1 round later Effect Duration=Permanent This is what's called a _love potion_ . It tastes and smells like alcohol, and takes effect not only by drinking it but also by smelling it (if it's only inhaled, its toxicity score is 9). Those who fail to resist will fall passionately in love with the first thing they see (regardless of gender or race). The only people you can fall in love with are fae, youma, titans, and mythical beasts that have human body parts (centaurs, etc.); you cannot fall in love with undead, animals, etc. The recipe for love passion has now been completely lost, so it's no longer possible to make it. There are only a few left from the ancient kingdom period, and they are traded at high prices. ## **[Living Doll (Injected/Magic)]** Effect=Turns women into little people and men into monsters Toxicity Score=13 Rarity=17 Effect Start Time=1 round left Effect Duration=Permanent This is a very rare type of poison, and only a few know the recipe. If a woman is wounded by a knife poisoned with this and fails to resist, her body will shrink and become a little person, about 30 cm tall, and will enter a state of suspended animation. The victims look like dolls, hence the name. 163 This poison has its normal effect only on women. When a man is affected by this poison and fails to resist, his body undergoes abnormal mutations as a side effect, turning him into a mutant monster. For mutant monster details, refer to page 226. ## **12.2.2** ## **Illnesses** This section summarizes the rules regarding illnesses. ## **How to Read Illness 12.2.2.1 Details** Illness details are expressed as follows. ## **[ Common Cold ]** Symptoms=Fever, cough, sneezing, runny nose, diarrhea, arthritis Rarity=5 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 day/5/7 (14) Fatal Depth=5 Infection Power=Moderate This is what they call a cold. It’s accompanied by a slight fever, cough, sneezing, runny nose, etc. Some people may experience diarrhea symptoms or joint pain. You suffer a -1 penalty on all success rolls while you have a cold. As the cold progresses, you may develop a high fever and also have difficulty breathing. ## 《 **Symptoms** 》 This briefly describes the conditions that appear in the illness. ## 《 **Rarity** 》 This score is used when making a check to see if you know the illness. If an owner of the sage skill or healer skill succeeds on a success roll using _skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score and **rarity** as the target score, they can look at a sick patient and identify which illness they have. This means not only knowing the name of the disease, but also its characteristics and how to deal with it. If a disease can be identified, owners of the healer skill can provide appropriate treatment. ## 《 **Progression Speed/Progression** ## **Intensity/ Healing Score** 》 After a certain period of time has elapsed after becoming ill, the illness either progresses further or gets better. The degree of progression of an illness is indicated by a score called **depth** . Immediately after contracting an illness, the _depth_ is 1. After the time indicated by **progression speed** has elapsed since you contracted the illness, roll two dice and look at the result. If the roll is less than the **progression intensity** , the disease gets worse and the _depth_ increases by 1. If the roll is greater than or equal to the _progression intensity_ but less than the **healing score** , the illness neither worsens nor improves, and the _depth_ remains the same. If the result is greater than or equal to the _healing score_ , the illness will be cured and the _depth_ will be reduced by 1. When the _depth_ becomes 0, you are completely recovered. The patient's adventurer level and life force bonus have _no_ influence on this check. Adventurer level may also be hero level, but _even if you're a hero, you cannot win against illness_ . Even if a shaman cannot use the spell _Restore Health_ , they can still work with one type of spirit to alleviate symptoms. For example, by working with salamander, fever, which is the main symptom of a cold, can be alleviated. When a shaman performs such a treatment, they gain a +1 bonus on progression/healing checks. In addition to these, depending on the illness, there are herbs and special medicines that can provide bonuses to progression/healing checks. When the holy magic _Cure Disease_ or _Refresh_ , or the spirit magic _Restore Health_ are cast, the caster must make a success roll using their magic power as the baseline score. The target score is double the _healing score_ (added in parentheses). If you succeed, the _depth_ of the illness is reduced by 1 and you can make an additional success roll. You can continue to make this check until you fail or until the illness is completely cured. If this does _not_ completely cure the illness, it’ll be _useless_ to cast these spells on the same patient until the caster's magic power increases. If a healer who knows the true nature of an illness cares for the patient for more than one hour per day, they can make a similar check using that healer's _healer skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score. However, this must be at each progression speed of the illness (i.e. immediately after the illness's progression check), and you can only make one success roll. However, even if they fail, the same healer can continue to care for the patient. If after some time has passed, an illness progression check occurs, the healer can also make a healing check. ## 《 **Fatal Depth** 》 If, as an illness progresses, its _depth_ reaches the **fatal depth** , the character will die from the illness. ## 《 **Infection Power** 》 Indicates the infectiousness of the illness. If it’s **very strong** , it has the infection power of a plague. **Strong** is about the same level as influenza. As in the example, **moderate** means it's just a cold, and **weak** means it's not very infectious. When it's **very weak** , it's not infectious and is unlikely to spread to other people. When an illness is _very strong_ or _strong_ , there is a high possibility that the infection will spread to the healer, etc. who is caring for the patient. If the game master determines that there is a possibility of contracting the illness, have the character make a progression check for the illness (compare a 2D roll with progression intensity). If they fail, 164 the character has contracted the illness at _depth_ 1. ## 《 **Description** 》 A detailed description and data on the illness is included at the end. ## **12.2.2.1 Illness Samples** Here are some examples of illnesses seen in _Sword World_ . Most of the illnesses listed here are incurable diseases that are difficult to heal and have a high mortality rate. The game master may also create any other illnesses they desire. **[ Green Slime Rot ]** Symptoms=Green spots appear on the body, which eventually rots away from there Rarity=15 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=3 days/6/9 (18) Fatal Depth=3 Infection Power=Moderate This is said to be an illness that occurs when the power of gnome in the body weakens and the power of undine becomes stronger. Immediately after infection, itchy green spots about 3 cm in diameter appear all over the body, but at _depth_ 2, the spots emit a putrid odor and a dirty pus begins to flow out. In this state, it’s almost impossible to take action. When the lethal depth of 3 is reached, the entire body rots, dissolves and washes away. The chance of being infected by a character before death is not particularly high, but if you touch the green goo left behind by a character after death, there's a very high chance of being infected. ## **[ Cotton Weaver ]** Symptoms=Cotton-like tissue peels off from the wound Rarity=14 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 week/5/10 (20) Fatal Depth=3 Infection Power=Weak This is a strange disease that occurs due to a significant decrease in the power of undine within the body. The cause is a very small wound on the fingertip, etc. When infected with this illness, the wound will not close at all, but moreover, something that looks like cotton will start to bulge out of the wound. At _depth_ 1, the character's actions are not affected at all. If you pluck a little bit of the cotton, it will instantly disappear, but at 2, the character will fall into a coma and the cotton will come out no matter how much you pluck. Although the progression speed is not that fast, it is an incurable disease that is difficult to treat once contracted. ## **[ Stone Skin ]** Symptoms=The surface of the body gradually becomes hard like stone Rarity=14 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=2 weeks/7/11 (22) Fatal Depth=4 Infection Power=Weak This is a terrifying illness in which the power of gnome becomes so strong that the surface of the body turns to stone beginning with the feet. At _depth_ 1, your ankles will become stiff and you won't be able to walk. At _depth_ 2, your knees become stiff. At _depth_ 3, your body will become stiff up to the waist, and at _depth_ 4, your chest will become stiff, at which point your character will be unable to breathe and will die of suffocation. However, the disease will continue to progress, and eventually your entire body will become a stone statue. ## **[ Dance Macabre ]** Symptoms=The body keeps dancing separate from the person's will Rarity=13 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=Half day/6/9 (18) Fatal Depth=4 Infection Power=Strong When a person contracts this illness, their body begins to dance on its own, regardless of their consciousness. Since the person him or herself feels festive while suffering from the illness, it’s believed that this is an abnormal manifestation of the power of leprechaun, which is a mind spirit. While suffering from this illness, you’ll suffer a -4 penalty to your attack power and evasion speed, and you will not be able to cast ancient magic or spirit magic. As the _depth_ of the illness increases, the dancing becomes more intense. **[ Deep Sleep ]** Symptoms=Excessive sleep, extreme fatigue Rarity=11 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=3 days/6/9 (18) Fatal Depth=5 Infection Power=Weak It’s believed that this illness is caused by the power of the mind spirit sandman becoming too strong. As the illness progresses, the patient will begin to complain of extreme fatigue with even the slightest amount of exercise, and the amount of time spent sleeping each day will increase (use _depth x 3 + 9_ as a standard). The patient will then die when it reaches the fatal depth of 5. The reason for the outbreak of the disease is unknown, but there are many known cases where excessive use of the spirit magic _Sleep_ has brought the disease to shamans. It may also be triggered by an unnatural sleep caused by the ancient magic _Sleep Cloud_ . 165 ## **[ Bat Fever ]** Symptoms=Fever Rarity=10 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=3 hours/6/8 (16) Fatal Depth=6 Infection Power=Weak This is an illness caused by bat bites. While you have this illness, you suffer a - 1 penalty on all success rolls due to fever. Infections are not often caused except by bat bites. ## **[ Balloon Burst ]** Symptoms=Shortness of breath, body swelling, inability to move, pain throughout the body Rarity=12 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=3 days/7/9 (18) Fatal Depth=3 Infection Power=Weak This illness causes difficulty breathing, along with body swelling, then you become unable to move, and eventually your body ruptures and you die. At depth 1, you suffer from severe breathing difficulties and will be unable to speak or cast spells. At depth 2, your body becomes abnormally swollen and round, and you'll be in severe pain and unable to move. If you reach the fatal depth of 3, your body will rupture and you will die. This illness is not seen in cold regions. Due to this, some argue that the spirit power of fire may also be involved in the onset of the illness. ## **[ Vampire Skin ]** Symptoms=Become extremely weak against sunlight Rarity=15 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 hour/6/9 (18) Fatal Depth=5 Infection Power=Weak This is an illness in which the power of dark spirits becomes unnecessarily strong, causing a hypersensitivity to light. At depth 1, painful blisters resembling burns accompany areas exposed to sunlight. At depth 2, vision is weakened and most success rolls suffer a -2 penalty. At depth 3 you lose your vision, and at depth 4 you fall into a coma. ## **[ Humbaba Curse ]** Symptoms=Extreme fever, vomiting, diarrhea, shortness of breath Rarity=16 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=2 days/7/10 (20) Fatal Depth=4 Infection Power=Very strong Characters affected by this illness brought on by the fire of the magical beast humbaba will suffer from severe fever and vomiting, making almost no activity possible. This disease is extremely contagious, classed as a plague. ## **[ Lycanthropy ]** Symptoms=Become a lycanthrope Rarity=8 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=Special/7/- (16) Fatal Depth=None Infection Power=Very weak This illness, which turns humans into lycanthropes, is thought to be different from normal illnesses caused by abnormalities in spirit power. Some sages claim that this is a type of curse (although these sages' claim is not accepted, as it cannot be removed with a _Remove Curse_ spell). Anyone wounded by a lycanthrope must roll 2D. If the roll is a 6 or less, you'll be infected with lycanthropy (the game master must secretly roll the dice if the adventurer does not know that lycanthropy is an infectious disease). The progression of lycanthropy differs from the rules of normal illnesses. Those infected with lycanthropy will have swollen wounds and a high fever. This fever continues endlessly. Every night from that day onwards, compare 2D + 3 with _the number of days since the new moon_ (if it's 16 days or more, then _30 - the number of days_ ). Once the score is equal to or less than the number of days, the character becomes a full lycanthrope and transforms into a beast form. This _cannot_ heal naturally. Before becoming a full lycanthrope, it can be cured with a spell such as the holy magic _Cure Disease_ , etc. (the target score is 16), but once you become a beast, you _cannot_ be cured again. ## **[ Rat Disease ]** Symptoms=Fever, swelling of joints Rarity=7 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 day/5/7 (14) Fatal Depth=5 Infection Power=Weak Those with this illness experience swelling in their joints and suffer from high fevers. If the _depth_ reaches 2 or greater, you will be unable to walk or stand. Infection occurs when bitten by a rat, but it's rarely transmitted from person to person. **[ Red Cap ]** Symptoms=Going berserk, change in body shape Rarity=12 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 week/5/10 (20) Fatal Depth=5 Infection Power=Very strong (Infection through gaze) When you're exposed to extremely strong fear, such as due to combat, etc. leprechaun, the mind spirit, manifests abnormally and causes this illness. When the illness is at depth 1, symptoms include being unable to move due to being seized by fear and having nightmares every night. When the depth is at 2 or greater, changes will appear in your body shape. You'll become hunched over, your hands abnormally long, your face contorted, and your eyes blazing red. This illness was given its name because the entire body turns red and hair falls out. Patients in this state go berserk and begin to attack things around them at random. Use the details p. 233 when fighting with these patients. This illness is transmitted when eye contact is made with someone who's been infected. If you make eye contact with someone who has the illness, a progression check must be made 166 immediately. In other words, roll two dice, and if the roll does not total 5 or greater, you’ll be infected. ## **[ Lockjaw ]** ## Symptoms=Extreme fever, shortness of breath ## Rarity=7 Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=2 days/8/9 (18) Fatal Depth=4 Infection Power=Very weak A wound can greatly disturb the balance of spirit power within the body, and the power of salamander in particular may become stronger. This results in lockjaw, a disease that causes a severe fever. A character will be unable to do much while suffering from lockjaw due to a high fever. It's not very contagious, but if you leave a wound with mud, etc. the chance of infection is fairly high. It is said that this is because undine and gnome create illusions due to the mud. **==> picture [164 x 191] intentionally omitted <==** ## **12.2.3 Herbs** Below is a list of **herbs** known in _Forcelia_ . Each has a provided rarity. Those with the sage, ranger, or healer skill can discern herbs by looking at them if they succeed on a success roll using rarity as the target score and _skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score. Each herb has a **base trade value** . If adventurers are in a large city, they can purchase them at the prices provided here. If an adventurer is lucky enough to obtain these items and sell them, a merchant will buy them for half the prices provided here. The prices are for one day's worth for one adult. You basically have to take them every day to receive a bonus on illness progression/healing checks. ## **[ Arnica ]** ## Rarity=10 Base Trade Value=600 gamels This is a perennial plant that’s often found in high mountains. Each plant has 2 to 3 flowers. The flowers can alleviate fever, and when used on a patient suffering from a fever, they'll receive a +2 bonus on progression/healing checks. One flower is enough for one day for an adult. The flowers can only be stored for 2 to 3 days. ## **[ Upas ]** ## Rarity=11 Base Trade Value=360 gamels This is a tall tree that has strong fire spirit power and can be found in areas with high average temperatures. The bark of this tree is a type of poison that causes feelings of vomiting. It's not useful for treating illness, but is effective when accidentally taking other poisons, giving you a +2 bonus to resistance rolls. However, it can be dangerous if used too much. A large amount of bark can be removed from one tree and can also be stored. ## **[ Dulcamara ]** ## Rarity=13 ## Base Trade Value=320 gamels This is a vine-like shrub that can be found in highlands. Its seeds are ground and mixed with oil to make an ointment that's used to treat skin diseases. When used on illnesses that cause severe inflammation of the skin, such as green slime rot, it provides a +2 bonus on progression/healing checks. Dozens of seeds can be harvested from one plant, and 2 to 3 seeds are enough for one adult per day. Seeds can be stored for long periods of time as long as they're in good condition. ## **[ Henruda ]** ## Rarity=15 ## Base Trade Value=9800 gamels This is an herb that grows in the wilderness and is the staple food of the cockatrice, a magical beast. This herb is useful as a preventative against monsters with the ability to petrify and _Petrification_ magic, but it's also a wonder drug for the dreaded _stone skin_ . If you take this herb daily, you'll receive a +4 bonus to progression/healing checks against _stone skin_ . One henruda plant is enough for one adult for one day. This herb cannot be stored and if it’s not used within three days of being collected, it will have no effect at all. For this reason, it’s rarely sold in town apothecaries. ## **[ Mandrake ]** ## Rarity=16 Base Trade Value=9000 gamels The roots of young mandrake are prized as an ingredient in sedatives. If used against an illness that stimulates going berserk, you’ll receive a +2 bonus on illness progression/healing checks. One mandrake is enough for one adult to take for one week. Depending on the refining process, mandrake can also be used as an ingredient for aphrodisiacs, poisons, etc. 167

# 12.3 Work Requests to NPCs

## **[ Lignia Stone ]** 

## Rarity=12 

Base Trade Value=12,000 gamels 

This is an amber stone that comes from the body of the mythical beast lynx. 

If you crush, brew, and drink this stone, you will receive a +4 bonus on progression/healing checks for illnesses that affect the mind (such as dance macabre). One lignia stone harvested 

from one Lynx can provide three days worth of medicine for one adult, and the stone as well as the powder crushed from it can be stored semi-permanently. 

## **12.3** 

## **Work Requests to NPCs** 

You want a high-level sage to appraise the treasure you found during your adventure. You found a beautiful gemstone and would like to have it forged into a ring. As you progress through your adventures, such things will happen. 

In these cases, adventurers can request NPCs to do this kind of work. If it’s a very simple task like those above, you can have it done for _NPC skill level x skill level x 30 gamels_ . For example, if you request an appraisal from a 5th level sage, you will have to pay 5 x 5 x 30 = **750** gamels. If the task becomes a little more complex, use numbers such as x _40_ or x _50_ instead of x 30. Also, if there’s no one 

available to do this for you, there is of course no point in paying any amount of money. 

If the task you're requesting from an NPC is something that will completely restrain that NPC, you will need to **hire an NPC** . In this case, higher compensation is required. The average price per day is about _NPC's skill level multiplied by 50 to 200 gamels_ . 

Please follow the standards on Table 11-2: NPC Presence by Level (see p. 154) to find out which skill level NPCs are in which locations. Of course, this depends on the type of skill. Even in villages, there are likely to be people with a fairly high farmer skill level (although 

it’s unlikely that you’ll ask such people for anything). 

For requests for such work, you'd usually give 10% to 30% as an advance payment. If the work _fails_ , you do _not_ have to pay the remainder. If the contractor willfully abandons the job, it is customary to return double the advance payment to the client. 

This is the price an _adventurer_ must pay when making a request to an _NPC_ . Adventurers _themselves_ cannot go around charging these same rates. If you don't have clients, you won't get paid (so just patiently wait for work at the adventurer shop, okay?).
